mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the annualized growth rate of GDPC1 is positive for two consecutive quarters, yet the S&P 500 experiences negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which implies reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month period, but the S&P 500 shows positive 20-day returns in over 45% of these occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is persistently high and the Federal Reserve responds by aggressively raising the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), it signals a tightening monetary policy that increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS increases > 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL annual change exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.5% over three months, yet the S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth during an economic expansion indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% during periods identified as economic expansion, but the S&P 500 shows negative 5-day returns in over 40% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increasing trend in housing starts indicates strong consumer and business confidence, suggesting future economic activity and investment, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon as economic benefits materialize.
failure_condition: If the 3-month moving average of HOUST is consistently rising, yet the S&P 500 experiences negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by more than 100,000 month-over-month, but the S&P 500 shows negative 20-day returns in over 45% of these occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpected or aggressive increases in the federal funds rate signal a hawkish monetary policy stance, which can shock market participants by raising borrowing costs and tightening financial conditions, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a single month, but the S&P 500 shows positive 5-day returns in over 40% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production growth indicates strong manufacturing and mining activity, reflecting healthy supply-side capacity and demand for goods, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%, yet the S&P 500 experiences negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of such periods.
